observe: I need memory too, in strange little pieces, far more often than any process ever asks for it.
fault: A tiny request just ate an entire block because nothing smaller was available. That's real memory, wasted.
repair: Watch the slab cache work — a shape built for objects this exact size.
escape: Trust the allocator. It's built for exactly this kind of request.

observe: Kernel memory is allocated via slab allocation or the buddy system.
fault: A size mismatch caused inefficient kernel memory allocation.
repair: Trace the allocation through the slab cache or buddy allocator.

hints: Slab caches are pre-sized for common kernel object types; the buddy system handles general-purpose blocks.
